summary: Iter21 (Driver Earnings Diagnose + Recompute) fully validated. 16/16 backend pytest cases passed covering all 11 review bugs: auth gating (no-token 401/403, user 403, empty email 400), unknown email returns empty profiles, case-insensitive duplicate detection (profiles_count=2 / is_duplicate=true), 3 completed rides (2.5+3.0+2.44=7.94€) surfaced as discrepancy_eur=7.94 vs stored=0, recompute dry_run=true returns 'would_recompute' WITHOUT persisting, recompute dry_run=false persists with payout_status='pending' & source='recomputed_by_admin', re-diagnose shows discrepancy=0, 'paid' months are correctly skipped (action='skipped_already_paid', sentinel 999.99 untouched), orphan rides (fake-orphan-id-iter21) surfaced in last-30-day window, validation 400 on missing/empty driver_id, 404 on unknown driver_id. Frontend Playwright via JWT admin bypass: AdminDashboard → Maintenance tab → 'open-driver-diagnostic-btn' opens the dialog with data-testid='driver-earnings-diagnostic-dialog'; email input + 'diagnose-run-btn' triggers GET /api/admin/diagnose/driver-earnings and renders the report card (Profils trouvés=0 toast on unknown email confirms full wiring). No backend/frontend errors observed.

minor.issue: Rides whose completed_at/created_at are missing or shorter than 7 chars are silently grouped under month_key 'unknown' and skipped (no action emitted). Acceptable but not surfaced in the response — consider emitting an 'action=skipped_no_date' entry so admin sees the count.

orphan_rides query only runs when profiles_count>0 (the if-profiles branch). If the searched email has zero matching drivers, orphans are not reported — minor gap, but documented behavior (returns []) and matches the spec.

issues: Radix Dialog console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent'. Cosmetic a11y warning, not blocking.

action_items: (Optional) Add a <DialogDescription> to DriverEarningsDiagnosticDialog for a11y (suppresses Radix warning).
(Optional) In recompute response, emit explicit 'skipped_no_date' actions for rides missing completed_at/created_at so admin sees the count instead of silent drop.
critical_code_review_comments: admin.py:3204 — regex search uses re.escape(email_clean) wrapped with ^...$ + $options:'i'. Correct and safely escapes regex metacharacters (good).

admin.py:3329 — month_key derived from completed_at[:7] OR created_at[:7]; if both missing, ride is grouped under 'unknown' and silently dropped from recompute. Not reported in actions[] — see minor issue above.
admin.py:3360 — upsert uses {$set: new_doc, $setOnInsert: {created_at: now_iso}} which is correct. Note: $set will also overwrite payout_status to 'pending' on existing 'pending' docs (intended).
DriverEarningsDiagnosticDialog.jsx:50 — POST /admin/recompute/driver-earnings uses axios with token; OK. After recompute it re-runs runDiagnose() which is the right UX.
MaintenanceTab.jsx:200 — DriverEarningsDiagnosticDialog rendered unconditionally with open prop. Fine since dialog returns null internally when !open via Radix Dialog. No memory leak risk.
DriverEarningsDiagnosticDialog.jsx — runRecompute uses window.confirm() for non-dry-run; works but inconsistent with rest of app that uses Radix AlertDialog. Cosmetic.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (dialog open + email input + diagnose API + report render verified)

test_credentials: Admin OTP-bypass via JWT signed with JWT_SECRET from /app/backend/.env (admin id from db.admins {email:'contact@metro-taxi.com'}). Same pattern as iter14/17/18/19/20.
seed_data_creation: All test seed prefixed 'test-iter21-' (drivers, rides, earnings) + driver_id='fake-orphan-id-iter21' for orphan ride. autouse module-scoped cleanup fixture wipes all of them after run. Verified post-run: drivers=0, ride_requests=0, driver_earnings=0 remaining.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter21 closed. Reusable artifact: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration21_driver_earnings_diag.py (16 tests, JWT admin bypass + auto-cleanup). Same JWT-injection-to-localStorage Playwright trick from iter20 keeps working under origin metro-taxi-demo.preview.emergentagent.com — token payload {user_id, role:'admin', exp} signed HS256 with JWT_SECRET. AdminDashboard route is /admin and the Maintenance tab data-testid is 'maintenance-tab'; new orange Card at top of MaintenanceTab exposes 'open-driver-diagnostic-btn' which opens dialog 'driver-earnings-diagnostic-dialog' with inputs 'diagnose-email-input' / 'diagnose-run-btn' and per-profile 'recompute-btn-<driver_id>'.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — new feature (diagnose + recompute). Feature correctly addresses the Maaz Tagari production case (a posteriori: if a duplicate driver profile exists from the 23/06 legacy import, the diagnose endpoint will list both profiles and pinpoint which one owns the orphaned rides; the recompute endpoint then rebuilds driver_earnings while preserving paid months). Both endpoints behave per spec.
